title: Build and Test an MCP Server with Official SDKs · Threat Case: Server Scaffold
summary: Implement a typed server with tools, resources, and automated protocol tests. Planned artifact: Add and verify the agent ext server scaffold increment in a runnable tested MCP server. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Create server metadata, capabilities, handlers, and transport entry points.
Select an official maintained SDK and pin a compatible protocol target.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Build Server Scaffold evidence
steps.instruction: Append a agent-ext-server-scaffold-oj13nn: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Server Scaffold into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
agent-ext-server-scaffold-oj13nn:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]

steps.title: Defend the Server Scaffold model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Server Scaffold applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Create server metadata, capabilities, handlers, and transport entry points.
